title: smilax elegans subsp. subrecta
intro: Smilax elegans subsp. subrecta, also known as the Natal or African greenbrier, is a climbing vine native to Southern Africa, characterized by its dark green, ovate leaves and fragrant white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Smilax elegans subsp. subrecta include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions, while also exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
